title: Waga Energy Posts 23% Growth in RNG Revenues for 2025
content: Waga Energy reported a 23% year-over-year increase in Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) production revenues, reaching â‚¬52.8 million for the full year 2025. Total consolidated revenue rose 7% to â‚¬59.6 million, with strong RNG sales offsetting a 55% decline in equipment sales due to project timing. The company produced 674 GWh (2.3 million MMBtu) of RNG, a 17% increase, avoiding 167,000 tons of CO2 emissions. As of early 2026, Waga operates 35 units globally with 95% availability and has 19 units under construction, totaling 3.7 TWh of capacity. Despite a softer market, the company maintains a robust pipeline of 213 projects representing 17.4 TWh. Waga Energy targets EBITDA breakeven during 2025, though its â‚¬200 million revenue goal has been shifted by roughly one year.
author: Analysts Account
